pi_items.source: Downloaded from https://wiki.wireshark.org/uploads/__moin_import__/attachments/SampleCaptures/smtp.pcap and verified with file command and tshark analysis
Verified in task.json and referenced in export_result.sh as target file path
Verified in task.json metadata field; task involves analyzing SMTP traffic for forensic evidence
tshark analysis of downloaded smtp.pcap: 'tshark -r smtp.pcap 2>/dev/null | wc -l' returns 60
tshark analysis: 'tshark -r smtp.pcap -Y "smtp" 2>/dev/null | wc -l' returns 32 SMTP packets

pi_items.metadata_value: Not claimed in metadata, but task requires extraction
pi_items.source: tshark extraction: 'tshark -r smtp.pcap -Y "smtp.req.command == MAIL" -T fields -e smtp.req.parameter' returns 'FROM: <gurpartap@patriots.in>'

pi_items.metadata_value: Not claimed in metadata, but task requires extraction
pi_items.source: tshark extraction: 'tshark -r smtp.pcap -Y "smtp.req.command == RCPT" -T fields -e smtp.req.parameter' returns 'TO: <raj_deol2002in@yahoo.co.in>'

pi_items.metadata_value: Not claimed in metadata, but task requires extraction
pi_items.source: tshark TCP stream follow analysis: 'tshark -r smtp.pcap -q -z "follow,tcp,ascii,0" 2>/dev/null | grep -i "^Subject:"' returns 'Subject: SMTP'

pi_items.metadata_value: Not claimed in metadata, but task requires extraction
pi_items.source: tshark extraction: 'tshark -r smtp.pcap -Y "smtp.response.code == 220" -T fields -e smtp.rsp.parameter' returns the server banner from 220 response code

privileged_info_summary: The smtp.pcap file is a real network capture from the official Wireshark Sample Captures collection (not synthetic), downloaded from wiki.wireshark.org. It contains exactly 60 total packets, with 32 SMTP protocol packets. The capture shows an SMTP email session from sender gurpartap@patriots.in to recipient raj_deol2002in@yahoo.co.in with subject line 'SMTP'. The SMTP server is xc90.websitewelcome.com running ESMTP Exim 4.69 software, based on the 220 greeting banner. All task metadata claims (pcap_file name, expected output filename, and task category) are accurate.
